_comment: Derived from Cervical Elongation_Medco.pdf and Cervical Elongation_PPD.CPD.pdf. Both PDFs contain the same clinical information in Part I. Part II and III for processing guidelines were taken from Cervical Elongation_PPD.CPD.pdf.
clinical_indications: The operation is indicated in congenital or nulliparous prolapse without cystocele where the cervix is pulled up mechanically through abdominal route

preauth.label: Detailed Clinical notes with history, indications, symptoms, signs, examination findings and advice for admission

ppd_preauth.q: Detailed Clinical notes – all vitals, detailed history, symptoms, signs, physical examination including local examination, indication for procedure, planned line of treatment, and advice for admission?
Did clinical presentation and imaging confirm the diagnosis?
Was there a documentation of previous prolapsed operation failure (upload previous operative notes)?

cpd_claim.q: Are the detailed ICPs with daily vitals and treatment details provided?
Are the detailed procedure / Operative Notes available?
Is the Discharge summary with follow-up advise at the time of discharge submitted?
Barcode of the Sling used if applicable?
Was the clinical presentation indicative of surgery?

common_queries: Surgery performed less than 6 weeks post-delivery or post-abortion.
Clinical presentation and imaging did not confirm the diagnosis.
Documentation of previous prolapsed operation failure is missing for revision surgery.

clinical_key_pointers: Abdominal sling operations have been designed for young women suffering from second or third degree uterine prolapse, and who are desirous of retaining their childbearing and menstrual functions.
contraindications: Less than 6 weeks post-delivery or post abortion
